qa_pairs.question: Does GROMACS automatically attempt to balance the load between Particle-Particle (PP) and Particle-Mesh-Ewald (PME) ranks?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, GROMACS's `mdrun` will automatically try to shift work from PP ranks to PME ranks by increasing the cutoff radius. This feature has been available since version 4.6.

qa_pairs.question: How can one disable the automatic PME tuning feature in GROMACS?
qa_pairs.answer: The automatic PME tuning, which `mdrun` performs, can be disabled by using the `mdrun` parameter `-notunepme`.

qa_pairs.question: Since which GROMACS version can PME calculations be offloaded to the GPU?
qa_pairs.answer: PME calculations can be offloaded to the GPU in GROMACS since version 2018.

qa_pairs.question: What is a limitation of the GPU-accelerated PME implementation in GROMACS version 2018.1?
qa_pairs.answer: In GROMACS version 2018.1, one of the limitations for GPU-accelerated PME is that only a single GPU rank can be dedicated to PME.
